title: On the Potential Role of Yui in NTE compared to NGE split
display_title: On the Potential Role of Yui in NTE compared to NGE split
wikitext:
[]: On the Potential Role of Yui in NTE compared to NGE [split]             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jul 03, 2014 4:18 am [Split from "Does FINAL even have a chance?" . - Monk] Sailor Star Dust wrote: I definitely agree with Rei 2 communicating to 3.0 Rei from Eva-01. My headcanon--unless we get something to the contrary--is at some point or another within Eva-01, Rei 2/Yui came up with some plans on how to eventually "set things right" (stopping Gendo/Seele's Instrumentality). Which can be inferred to an extent from EoE with Lilith/Adam/Yui. I was just throwing a different thought process out there.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 03, 2014 8:30 am ElMariachi wrote: Now they'll only have to hope that Yui have benevolent intentions, and don't plan let Instrumentality be launched or worse, had been on Gendo's side all along!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jul 03, 2014 9:25 am ^ Well that's a fancy and half-crazy theory I have since some time that there are some elements that could imply that Yui and Gendo are in cahoots in the Rebuild: - first there's the moment Gendo spaces out during his dinner with Rei and instead see Yui during a brief moments. Her words, "Honey please, take care of Shinji" sounds a lot like a last request, something you said before being separated for a long time (or even forever). Knowing Gendo, such words would had immediately triggered red flags on Gendo's mind, especially if she said that a few time before the Contact Experiment. In NGE Yui was so afraid that Gendo discovers what she planed to do that she only confided herself to Fuyutsuki and instructed him to never told Gendo the truth, that's a pretty brutal difference in cautiousness here. - second, the revelation in 3.0 that Ayanami is Yui's maiden name makes a very powerful symbol of the name Gendo gave to Rei: in NGE it was only a random name he gave because she had to have one, but in Rebuild he gave her Yui's name, implying that he sees her like a legitimate daughter, and we know that Gendo sacralized everything regarding Yui, so I don't think that he would had given to Rei the original name of his dear wife if Gendo made Rei "behind Yui's back", ie that Yui didn't voiced her approval. Those two points lead me to think that Yui put Gendo in the confidence of what she was about to do, or even that they devised that plan together, and that they planned that it would result in Rei's "birth" as a side effect, which this is why Gendo semi-officially made her part of the family by giving her Yui's maiden name. And just imagine Misato and the rest of WILLE's face when they realize that their "Ship of Hope" is actually working for the other side! That could also imply that Yui was helping Gendo to snatch Shinji away in 3.0: that could explain how Mark.09 could stay undetected until the last second (Yui tampering with the radar) or why the DSS Choker didn't seemed to worry Rei Q, or at least hasn't been instructed to worry about it and kill Misato before she could detonate it (had Misato pressed the button, Yui, indirectly controlling the Magi, would had aborted the order, and probably made the wunder tilt slightly to throw Misato overboard! )

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 03, 2014 9:55 am I assumed Yui's "last request" we heard in 2.0 was Yui saying that as a general statement ("in case something happens to me, take care of Shinji") or Gendo imagining if Yui was there instead of Rei, that's what she would have said. Yui being in cahoots with Gendo would be an interesting twist (meaning she wants Instrumentality or they both want a positive outcome like EoE*?), but it just seems so against her very personality in either continuity so far. *Everything Gendo is doing so far is very negative ("He's paying with his very soul"), that I can't imagine it'll turn out he's trying to do something that'll benefit humanity in the end. This is getting off-topic, so I'll split/merge things later today if I'm not beaten to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Jul 03, 2014 2:55 pm ChaddyManPrime wrote: @ Baggy Exactly, none of that lame ass time travel crap is necessary, though I don't technically count Omnipresence time travel.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 03, 2014 3:03 pm I might have mentioned this around the time of 2.0, but if Instrumentality is shown in Final, I'd love for it to be in a positive (instead of negative light) as a nice contrast to EoE and hopefully Shinji's growth. Like, it's rejected because the concept still equals suicide/loss of individuality, but the process is positive. No stranglings, just gradual acceptance.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu Jul 03, 2014 9:25 pm ^ I really don't know how to feel about Instrumentality, the closest I can come to say something about it is it all depends on the eye beholder to make it into a heaven or a hell.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jul 03, 2014 10:48 pm In both original endings, Shinji fails the test of the Void. In EoTV he succumbs to solipsistic suicide and gradual dissolution, ala a dying patient fading out on a heavy dose of morphine. In EoE he's shattered by the abyss. That's not going to happen. I don't see anything in 90 minutes to turn his character around to the point that he's pulling everyone else out rather than undergoing group therapy. Given that each film in the series has had a distinct theme, I don't think we can really say what will happen with any real clarity. Each new film has been a drastic break from the mood and character of its predecessor. Final will probably continue that pattern. It won't be an Q part 2, it'll be Final.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 04, 2014 4:57 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: I assumed Yui's "last request" we heard in 2.0 was Yui saying that as a general statement ("in case something happens to me, take care of Shinji") or Gendo imagining if Yui was there instead of Rei, that's what she would have said. Yui being in cahoots with Gendo would be an interesting twist (meaning she wants Instrumentality or they both want a positive outcome like EoE*?), but it just seems so against her very personality in either continuity so far. *Everything Gendo is doing so far is very negative ("He's paying with his very soul"), that I can't imagine it'll turn out he's trying to do something that'll benefit humanity in the end.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 04, 2014 7:43 am ElMariachi wrote: Yui's characterization was very vague: we know that she genuinely loved her son and husband, was fiercely protective of the former, and genuinely believed in what she fought for. But on the other hand, she was raised by SEELE, and her plan was to "ascend" into an eternal monument of mankind, which have to include Instrumentality to reach her goal, meaning that she never intended to try to stop it from happening, with all the death and suffering that will come with it.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 04, 2014 10:04 am ^ Yeah, and to suck Rei's god juice, Rei has to become a goddess first, and for that she needs to do the Forbidden Union, which trigger an Impact (and Instrumentality if someone is controlling it), so maybe she planned something to have Instrumentality be stopped (he dialog in EoE suggest that she too thought that it wasn't good for mankind), but it doesn't change the fact that it happening is part of her plan to become that goddess/monument, if only as a side-effect of Rei's ascension to godhood.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Fri Jul 04, 2014 10:14 am ^ You know I just thought of something and I don't remember if it was ever explained. Why didn't they just put Lilith's soul into Unit 01 alongside Yui's? Gendo could have just walked up to Unit 01 with Adam in his hand and touched it, and bam 3rd Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 04, 2014 10:38 am ElMariachi wrote: ^ Yeah, and to suck Rei's god juice, Rei has to become a goddess first, and for that she needs to do the Forbidden Union,

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Fri Jul 04, 2014 10:46 am ^ Adam's soul? Who and how did they retrieve Kaworu's soul? Did the soul just automatically go into Gendo's hand after Kaworu died? The other Angels participated in 3rd Impact?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jul 04, 2014 11:00 am ChaddyManPrime wrote: ^ Why didn't they just put Lilith's soul into Unit 01 alongside Yui's? Gendo could have just walked up to Unit 01 with Adam in his hand and touched it, and bam 3rd Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 04, 2014 11:02 am ChaddyManPrime wrote: ^ Adam's soul? Who and how did they retrieve Kaworu's soul? Did the soul just automatically go into Gendo's hand after Kaworu died?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jul 04, 2014 12:18 pm Yui let Unit 02 be destroyed because she's a sociopath and SEELE wanted all the angels dead so they wouldn't muck up the process. Don't want Ramiel turning everybody into crystals or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 04, 2014 12:23 pm Chuckman wrote: Yui let Unit 02 be destroyed because she's a sociopath and SEELE wanted all the angels dead so they wouldn't muck up the process. Don't want Ramiel turning everybody into crystals or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jul 04, 2014 12:36 pm Reject the Saint Yui cult and come to accept that she is literally figuratively Hitler. Your interpretation of the show's metaphysics is as made up as mine. SEELE had to destroy the angels first for some reason and compassion for them is... lame. Who cares about flying death crystals? Yeesh.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 04, 2014 12:40 pm Chuckman wrote: Reject the Saint Yui cult and come to accept that she is literally figuratively Hitler.
